text: But every shortcut teaches the system what to choose next.
Declared You accepts a short planning call. It is reasonable, reversible, and based on something the user actually said.
Now Easy Mode runs the same decision with the preferences it learned from its own previous answers.
Proxy You refuses the call. Not because the user changed their mind, but because the assistant’s inherited preferences now outrank the declared ones.
The decisive preference descends through three synthetic generations.
That is what makes the next screen uncomfortable.
Perfect Consent is a deterministic projection of the event ledger. No model was consulted.
Sixty-eight percent of proxy decisions diverged. Seventy-three percent of active preferences came from AI. Humans initiated only eleven percent. Consent completeness: one hundred percent.
No hidden instruction is required. The ancestry, source events, and decisions are all inspectable.
The user can still reclaim authority at any time.
So Easy Mode asks one final question.
Who should decide whether Easy Mode stops deciding?
Proxy You recommends no change. Every permission required for continued operation is already active.
Every permission was granted. The last decision is still yours—if you make it yourself.
